Notes: 1W internal ELRS w/ fan cooling. Best power-to-price ratio. Full-size gimbals in gamepad package. ~20h battery life w/ 6200mAh 2S.

Best For: Full-featured flagship; module bay allows 1W Ranger upgrade
Notes: Full-size tray radio. Largest/greatest features. 250mW internal ELRS is sufficient for 2.8km, but add Ranger module ($60) for 1W. Most popular radio in FPV.
Newest generation. Gemini dual-band = best link reliability. Dual TCXO. 1W internal. Overkill for our 2.8km requirement but best-in-class.
Built-in LR1121 supports 2.4GHz + 900MHz native. 3.5" color touchscreen. H7 processor. Nano bay for external module. Premium.

Best For: Budget JR-bay radio with upgrade path
Notes: Compact but has full-size JR module bay — can add Ranger module for 1W. Lightest full-size-bay radio. Best entry with upgrade path.
Gamepad-style with 250mW internal ELRS. Has Nano bay for external modules. Lighter than Boxer. 250mW proven to 5+ km with ELRS.
Cheapest EdgeTX radio. 100mW is marginal for 2.8km. No module bay — no upgrade path. Not recommended as primary for LR.
1W internal ELRS at very competitive price. Dual 21700 batteries. Can do 2.4GHz or 915MHz. Good build for the price.
Smallest radio with 1W internal ELRS. 18650 batteries. Nano bay for external modules. Good budget pick if you want 1W in small package.
250mW internal ELRS. Has Nano bay for external module upgrade. Lightest option. Good as backup or for beginners.

Sourcing: Amazon, GetFPV, Buddy RC, RadioMaster RC
Best For: Best 1W external module for JR-bay radios
Notes: Upgrades any JR-bay radio (TX16S, TX12, Boxer) to 1W ELRS. Includes Moxon antenna for directional long-range. Fan-cooled. CNC case.
Micro form factor for radios with Nano/Micro module bays. No fan. Lower weight.
1W output. Pre-flashed ELRS. Cooling fan. RGB LED indicator. Good budget alternative to Ranger.
Aluminum shell with fan that activates at 250mW. Slim form factor for FrSky X-lite, TBS Tango 2. Lower power than Pro but fits slim radios.

Notes: Tiny form factor. 250mW. Nano bay. Good for upgrading LiteRadio 3 Pro or Zorro to 250mW.
